papers.title_en: RL-Index: Reinforcement Learning for Retrieval Index Reasoning
papers.authors: Yongjia Lei, Nedim Lipka, Zhisheng Qi, Utkarsh Sahu, Koustava Goswami, Franck Dernoncourt, Ryan A. Rossi, Yu Wang
papers.evidence: 利用强化学习以智能体方式改进检索索引推理，可应用于智能体能力的后训练增强
papers.tldr: 现有检索系统在处理需要隐式复杂推理的查询时，常依赖查询端改写，导致在线延迟高且未利用索引端推理能力。本文提出RL-Index，将索引推理建模为强化学习，在索引阶段用LLM生成理由增强文档，并通过GRPO和检索相似度奖励直接优化索引。在BRIGHT基准上，RL-Index显著提升检索和问答性能，同时降低在线延迟。该方法可作为即插即用策略，跨不同检索器和生成器泛化。
papers.abstract_en: Retrieving external knowledge is essential for solving real-world tasks, yet it remains challenging when the relationship between a query and its relevant knowledge involves implicit and complex reasoning beyond surface-level semantic or lexical matching (e.g., mathematical problems relying on the same theorem or coding requiring deep reasoning). Existing approaches primarily rely on query-side reasoning (e.g., query rewriting), which introduces significant online latency and underutilizes the opportunity to perform reasoning over the knowledge corpus itself (i.e., index-side reasoning). In this paper, we propose RL-Index, an agentic indexing framework that formulates retrieval index reasoning as a reinforcement learning problem. Instead of performing reasoning at query time, RL-Index shifts reasoning to the indexing stage by augmenting documents with LLM-generated rationales that explicitly encode the latent query-knowledge relationship. To optimize the quality of these rationales, we employ Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO) and use retrieval similarity as a verifiable reward signal, enabling direct optimization of indexing decisions for retrieval effectiveness. Extensive experiments on the BRIGHT benchmark demonstrate that RL-Index consistently improves both retrieval and downstream question-answering performance, while significantly reducing online inference latency. Moreover, the learned rationale augmentation generalizes across diverse retrievers and generators, highlighting its robustness as a plug-and-play indexing strategy across different retrieval systems.

papers.title_en: SAGE: Stochastic Prompt Optimization via Agent-Guided Exploration
papers.authors: Ziyi Zhu, Luka Smyth, Saki Shinoda, Jinghong Chen
papers.evidence: 智能体引导的提示优化作为自主智能体的后训练改进
papers.tldr: 上下文工程已成为提升人工智能系统的关键，但文本梯度无法直接用于提示优化，需将其视为黑盒搜索。我们提出随机提示优化框架SPO，并比较错误驱动随机搜索、遗传算法和智能体引导探索SAGE三种策略，其中SAGE引入诊断代码执行。在三个基准上，无单一策略绝对占优，效果取决于任务景观与错误类型；SAGE在心理健康聊天机器人中通过八轮A/B测试复合，显著提升次日留存。该研究揭示了定性诊断与定量验证的结合是智能体优化在开放式对话中有效的关键。
papers.abstract_en: Context engineering has emerged as a primary lever for improving AI systems without parameter updates. Recent work showing that textual gradients do not function as real gradients motivates treating automatic prompt optimization (APO) as black-box search. We introduce SPO (Stochastic Prompt Optimization), a framework for stochastic search over prompt space, and compare three strategies of increasing sophistication: error-informed random search, a genetic algorithm with evolutionary operators, and SAGE (SPO via Agent-Guided Exploration), a multi-agent pipeline with diagnostic code execution. Across three benchmarks, no single strategy dominates; effectiveness depends on the interaction of landscape structure with error type. We further deploy SAGE on a mental-health chatbot under a continuous optimization paradigm, where it compounds eight cycles of individually-noisy A/B tests into a statistically robust gain in next-day retention. We argue that coupling qualitative diagnosis with quantitative validation is what makes agentic optimization effective for open-ended task-oriented dialogue.

papers.title_en: AI Economist Agent: An Agentic Framework for Model-Grounded Economic Analysis with RAG, Knowledge Graphs, and Large Language Models
papers.evidence: 基于RAG和知识图谱的AI经济学家智能体用于经济分析
papers.tldr: 经济学家在场景分析中需要基于经济理论与实证数据的主张，但现有大型语言模型多只能生成表面流畅的经济叙述。为此，本文提出基于检索增强生成（RAG）与知识图谱的AI经济学家智能体框架，将经济数据与理论知识图谱整合，使用LLM智能体进行任务规划、相关证据检索、分析模型选择，生成并以显式模型计算为基础的经济叙述，而非纯文本输出。在美联储政策与通胀持续性报告生成及商业地产再融资银行压力测试叙述生成两项应用中，所生成报告的经济连贯性与证据可追溯性显著提升。该工作为结合经济模型与AI智能体的可解释分析提供了新范式。
papers.abstract_en: We propose a model-grounded RAG-based AI economist with an agentic framework for economic scenario analysis using large language models (LLMs) and knowledge graphs. While LLMs can generate fluent economic narratives, economists are often required to make economic claims grounded by economic theory and real-world data. Based on this motivation, this study proposes an RAG-based AI economist, which utilizes knowledge graphs including economic data and theory and LLM-based agents to plan the analysis, retrieve relevant evidence, select appropriate models, and generate reports. In our framework, we do not produce quantitative claims directly with the language model alone; instead, we generate narratives grounded in explicit model-based computations and linked to the retrieved evidence via AI agents. We refer to our framework as an AI economist agent. We evaluate the AI economist agent in two applications: economist report generation for U.S. inflation persistence and Federal Reserve policy, and bank stress-test narrative generation for U.S. commercial real estate refinancing stress. The results illustrate how grounding the generated reports improves their economic coherence and traceability.
